records.ety: [OE. flaterie, OF. flaterie, F. flaterie, fr. flater to flatter, F. flatter; of uncertain origin. See Flatter, v. t.]

defs: The act or practice of flattering; the act of pleasing by artful commendation or compliments; adulation; false, insincere, or excessive praise.
quotes: Just praise is only a debt, but flattery is a present.
Flattery corrupts both the receiver and the giver.
